name: democratic_legitimacy_support
description: Measures the degree to which citizens value, trust, and are satisfied with democratic governance in Mexico, capturing both normative commitment to democracy and evaluations of its actual functioning.

name: institutional_trust_and_electoral_confidence
description: Captures citizens' trust in key political and social institutions (police, neighbors, INE) and their confidence in the integrity of electoral processes, reflecting broader faith in the formal rules of the political game.

name: political_efficacy_and_engagement
description: Measures citizens' sense that politics is relevant to their lives, that they can influence government decisions, and their subjective interest in and comprehension of political affairs.

name: authoritarian_predisposition
description: Taps into citizens' preference for strong executive leadership over legal constraints, tolerance for bypassing rules to achieve just ends, and willingness to subordinate individual freedoms to security or order.

name: perceived_political_influence_of_actors
description: Measures citizens' perceptions of how much influence various political and social actors—including the president, parties, businesses, media, unions, the Church, and ordinary citizens—exert over Mexican politics.
